rationale: The boundary violation I missed in round 1 is now correctly fixed. Verified at 389fcc41: (1) internal/approve no longer imports internal/gitutil (`go list -f '{{.Imports}}'` empty for gitutil); the two read-only calls route through new thin wrappers lifecycle.IsAncestor/BranchExists in internal/lifecycle/gitquery.go. lifecycle is NOT one of the 5 ADR-0030 enforcement packages (validate, approve, complete, state, phase per boundary_test.go:111-117) and already legitimately imports gitutil — architecturally correct. TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks PASSES. (2) NO `boundary-allowlisted:` exception was added — the boundary is honored, not exempted. (3) No cycle: `go list -deps ./internal/lifecycle` contains no internal/approve; approve→lifecycle and approve→complete edges intact. (4) Seam-transparent: wrapper signatures are identical to gitutil's (IsAncestor(workdir,ancestor,descendant)(bool,error); BranchExists(name)bool); only the two injection-point assignments + comments changed, so gate behavior and all ACs are byte-identical — my round-1 ADR/blast-radius conclusions still hold. go build ./... clean; approve tests pass; git tree clean (only the untracked reviews dir).

findings.note: approve→gitutil import GONE; rerouted through lifecycle.IsAncestor/BranchExists (gitquery.go). Confirmed via go list -f Imports (no gitutil) — the exact violation I missed in round 1.
TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks passes; lifecycle absent from the 5-package enforcement set (boundary_test.go:111-117), so its gitutil access is legitimate.
No boundary-allowlisted: exception in the fix diff — boundary HONORED not exempted.
Acyclic: lifecycle deps exclude internal/approve; approve→lifecycle/complete edges preserved. No new cycle from the wrapper.
Wrapper signatures identical to gitutil originals; seam names/types/injection points unchanged → gate behavior + AC1-13 unaffected.
